name: Quadraphonic Mixer
description: This algorithm is based on the disting EX algorithm of the same name. It is a quadraphonic mixer – that is, it has four outputs for four speakers, rather than the usual two for a stereo mixer. The algorithm provides two means to perform that classic quadraphonic trick, spinning the audio around the audience. Each channel has a Spinner and an Orbiter. The spinner internally modulates the Angle parameter and only works if the channel is using polar coordinates, spinning the position around the centre. The orbiter spins the audio position around the nominal position set by the coordinates and works in both polar and rectangular coordinate modes.

parameters.name: Output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above, for all outputs.

parameters.name: Overall gain
parameters.description: An overall gain control; combines with the front and rear gain controls.

parameters.name: Front gain
parameters.description: A gain control for the front speakers.

parameters.name: Rear gain
parameters.description: A gain control for the rear speakers.

parameters.name: Front bass
parameters.description: A shelving cut/boost bass tone control for the front speakers.

parameters.name: Front treble
parameters.description: A shelving cut/boost treble tone control for the front speakers.

parameters.name: Rear bass
parameters.description: A shelving cut/boost bass tone control for the rear speakers.

parameters.name: Rear treble
parameters.description: A shelving cut/boost treble tone control for the rear speakers.

parameters.name: Gain
parameters.description: The channel’s gain.

parameters.name: Coordinates
parameters.description: Whether the channel uses Rectangular or Polar coordinates.

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: If using Rectangular coordinates, the channel’s X position, from left (-100%) to right (100%).

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: If using Rectangular coordinates, the channel’s Y position, from rear (-100%) to front (100%).

parameters.name: Angle
parameters.description: If using Polar coordinates, the channel’s angle, where 0 is front centre and ±180 is rear centre.

parameters.name: Radius
parameters.description: If using Polar coordinates, the channel’s radius, where 0% is centre and 100% is the maximum distance from the centre.

parameters.name: Spinner
parameters.description: Which channel’s spinner to use.

parameters.name: Spin rate
parameters.description: The rate of spin to apply.

parameters.name: Orbiter
parameters.description: Which channel’s orbiter to use.

parameters.name: Orbit rate
parameters.description: The rate of the orbiter.

parameters.name: Orbit radius
parameters.description: The radius of the orbiter.

parameters.name: Orbit phase
parameters.description: Sets the phase angle to add to the orbiter. Different inputs with the same orbiter but different phase follow each other around the circle at a fixed offset.

parameters.name: Mute
parameters.description: Mutes the channel.

parameters.name: Solo
parameters.description: Solos the channel.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: A text name for the channel for identification.

parameters.name: Distance gain
parameters.description: Sets the amount by which the gain will change when moving away from the centre. For example, if the listener is notionally in the centre position, you may like the audio to get quieter as the source moves away from the centre, in which case set this to a negative value.

output_ports.name: Front left output
output_ports.description: The bus to use for the front left speaker output.

output_ports.name: Front right output
output_ports.description: The bus to use for the front left right output.

output_ports.name: Rear left output
output_ports.description: The bus to use for the rear left speaker output.

output_ports.name: Rear right output
output_ports.description: The bus to use for the rear right speaker output.
